topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The lukewarm coffee in my hand suddenly felt like acid. I stared at the man across the crowded gallery, his easy smile directed entirely at me, a smile that felt utterly misplaced. It was impossible. The way he tilted his head when he laughed, the specific, slightly crooked line of his jaw—it was all too familiar. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I watched him move toward the sculpture, his movements fluid and familiar, and a sharp, unpleasant feeling washed over me. *This can’t be happening.* I took a deliberate sip of my drink, trying to keep my expression neutral, but my jaw was tight. He turned back, catching my eye, and for a split second, his expression shifted—a flicker of recognition that made my skin prickle with irritation.

[story 2]

"Excuse me, but I think we've met before," I managed, my voice clipped and brittle. The woman across the table blinked, looking genuinely bewildered. I leaned back, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, the gesture defensive and closed off. Every detail—the way she tucked a stray strand of hair behind her ear, the subtle scent of sandalwood clinging to her sweater—screamed familiarity. It was infuriating. I felt a distinct tightening in my chest, a restless energy bubbling up that I desperately wanted to suppress. I watched her try to process my accusation, and the slight furrow in her brow only fueled my internal judgment. *How could she possibly not remember?*

[story 3]

I watched him across the dimly lit bar, the way he leaned against the mahogany, radiating an effortless charm that grated on my nerves. He was laughing at something one of the other patrons said, and the sound of his voice—that particular, low timbre—sent a spike of annoyance through me. I shifted my weight, subtly angling my body away from him, pulling my jacket tighter around myself as if to create distance. My gaze was fixed on the precise angle of his shoulder, cataloging every familiar feature. A sharp, involuntary sigh escaped me, laced with palpable impatience. I wanted to walk over, demand an explanation, but I held back, letting the silent tension between us become a heavy, unwelcome thing.
